specification.topic-statement: The root agent bootstrap system ensures exactly one root agent exists in the database, creating it on first launch and adopting any messages that predate the agent tracking era.

scope.in-scope: Detection of whether a root agent exists, creation of the root agent record, orphaned message adoption, and insertion of the initial fork event.
scope.boundary: SQL execution, row insertion, and transaction context are delegated to the database layer. The caller is responsible for any surrounding transaction.
How the root agent's conversation history is replayed into memory at startup is handled by the agent session restoration subsystem.

How the effective system prompt is assembled from configuration and pinned files is out of scope. The bootstrap records the path to the system prompt file as part of the initial fork event but does not read or assemble the prompt itself.

field.name: name
field.note: always null at creation; no display name assigned

field.name: parent identifier
field.note: always null; distinguishes the root from all other agents

field.name: created at
field.note: Unix timestamp as a decimal string

field.name: data
field.note: contains role='child' and pinned_paths array with the system prompt file path

behavior.checking-for-existing-root:
The database is queried for an agent with no parent. The query selects the identifier of the first such agent.
If a row is returned, that identifier is returned to the caller and all subsequent steps are skipped. The operation is idempotent: calling it when a root agent already exists has no side effects.
behavior.creating-the-root-agent: If no root agent exists, a new unique identifier is generated. A new agent record is inserted with that identifier, null name, null parent, status "running", the current Unix timestamp, fork message id zero, and the provided session identifier.
behavior.orphaned-message-adoption:
Databases created before agent tracking was introduced may contain messages that have no associated agent identifier. The bootstrap checks whether the agent identifier column exists on the messages table before proceeding, as a guard against running on a schema version that predates that column.
If the column exists, the database is checked for any messages whose agent identifier is null. If any exist, they are all updated to assign them to the newly created root agent.
The column-existence check is defensive: the bootstrap behaves correctly both on a fresh schema and on a schema that has not yet been migrated to include the agent identifier column.
All null-agent messages are adopted by the root agent in a single bulk update. No filtering or selection is applied.
behavior.inserting-initial-fork-event:
An initial fork event is inserted into the root agent's conversation history. The event's data payload records that the root agent was created with a system prompt, and includes the file path of the system prompt document as the sole entry in the pinned paths list.
This event serves as the starting point for agent restoration: the session restoration subsystem uses fork events to reconstruct which documents were pinned when an agent was created.
behavior.returning-the-identifier: The root agent's identifier (whether retrieved from an existing record or generated during creation) is returned to the caller.
behavior.error-behavior: If any database query or insertion fails, an error is returned and no further steps are attempted. On error, any temporary allocations are released. Partial state (for example, a successfully inserted agent row followed by a failed message adoption) is left in place; no rollback is performed by this function. The caller is responsible for transaction management.
